marketState: neutral - near-term weather-driven demand spike offsets a bearish medium-term storage/seasonality backdrop
summary: Front-month is trading essentially at fair value ($2.888 vs $2.917 fair, spread -$0.029, status FAIR) but sits well below its typical seasonal path YTD (-15.2% vs -3.4% median, -11.8pp), a discount that lines up with a persistent storage surplus (+184 Bcf vs 5y, latest print a bearish +72 Bcf surprise) and still-net-short managed money (-60,295 contracts, ratio 0.8, 35th percentile). Seasonality gives only a 31% chance of higher prices in four weeks with a typical -3.1% move, but the model flags reliability as Low (50pp spread in outcomes), so that skew alone isn't strong enough to force a medium-term trade against the fair-value curve, which is itself drifting higher into autumn/winter ($2.917 now to $3.040 by +21D) in line with steep futures-curve contango (Jan 2027 near $4.26). Over the next 1-7 days demand is running +25.9% above the 5-year norm with rangePos above the historical max (aboveMax=true) and still climbing (+13.6 in 24h), a genuine near-term bullish catalyst the 8-16 day outlook doesn't yet confirm (+8.2%, rangePos 0.68, unremarkable). No technicals were available this run (empty dataset), removing a normal cross-check and lowering conviction on both ideas.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality (probHigher 4w only 31%, typical move -3.1%) and a bearish storage surprise (+72 Bcf, +184 Bcf vs 5y) argue for downside, but reliability is explicitly Low and the fair-value curve is rising into winter ($2.917 now to $3.040 by +21D) alongside steep futures contango, so the medium-term picture is genuinely mixed rather than a clean edge in either direction.
longTerm.trigger: Short bias firms if front rallies into the upper half of the fair-value band (~$3.15-$3.40) without a supporting storage deficit, or if another 1-2 bearish storage surprises confirm the surplus trend and mmNet percentile rises. Long bias firms if seasonality reliability improves and probHigher(4w) moves back above 50%, or 8-16D weather demand strengthens meaningfully above +8.2%.

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; ~$0.10/MMBtu stop distance ≈ $1,000/contract (10,000 MMBtu lot) - size to keep that at 1% of equity
shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D demand is +25.9% above the 5-year norm with rangePos above the historical max (aboveMax=true) and still rising (+13.6 in 24h), a genuine near-term tightening signal, while front price is only modestly discounted to fair value (spread -$0.029, status FAIR) so there's room to re-price toward or through fair value on continued heat. Futures curve shows the front slightly inverted to next (-$0.035), consistent with near-term demand pulling the prompt contract up.
shortTerm.invalidation: A cooling in the 1-7D weather demand reading back toward the 5y norm, or price closing below $2.78, would remove the catalyst and invalidate the setup.

keyDrivers: Storage surplus +184 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build - medium-term oversupply pressure
Near-term weather demand +25.9% vs 5y, rangePos above historical max, still rising - short-term bullish catalyst
Seasonality shows only 31% odds of higher price in 4 weeks and typical -3.1% move, but flagged Low reliability
Front price trading FAIR vs fair value ($2.888 vs $2.917) while fair value itself rises into winter ($3.040 by +21D)
Managed money net short (-60,295, ratio 0.8) at 35th percentile - bearish lean but not extreme positioning
risks: Technicals dataset was empty this run - no chart-based confirmation for either trade
